channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These 5 clever mapping upstarts do what Google Maps can’t
item.description: Look, Google Maps is fine. It’s arguably even great—probably one of the most-opened apps on lots of our phones and absolutely one of the most indispensable.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Teamsters say Amazon workers will strike at multiple facilities as union seeks labor contract
item.description: The International Brotherhood of Teamsters said workers at seven Amazon facilities will begin a strike on Thursday morning, an effort by the union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Snapchat is overhauling how influencers earn money on the platform
item.description: “I’m sorry WHO is using snapchat in the big year of 2024,” one X user asked. Turns out, a lot of people and for some, it’s proving lucrative. Influencer Julieanna Goddard, better known as YesJulz, recently posted her impressive Snapchat earnings to social media in response to the question.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: VA EHR in better shape to resume go-lives in FY 2025 after ‘incremental’ fixes
item.description: VA’s Oracle-Cerner EHR has seen a 50% decrease in crashes and lags since the VA completed its last round of updates in August. The post VA EHR in better shape to resume go-lives in FY 2025 after ‘incremental’ fixes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CompTIA launches expert-level cybersecurity certification
item.description: CompTIA this week made available its CompTIA SecurityX certification program, which is an updated version of the CompTIA Advanced Security Practitioner (CASP+) cert and part of CompTIA’s Xpert Series of certifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Brain Rot: Experts Warn Excessive Social Media Use Harming Teens' Mental Health
item.description: Experts warn that excessive social media use is harming teens' mental health and creating health concerns.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Youtube has a new tool to detect AI-faked celebrities
item.description: Fake AI clips of celebrities are becoming increasingly common, something that affects, among other things, artists and actors who have their voices and looks cloned without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: MacOS Sequoia 15.2 and the Frustrating Reality of Third-Party Backups
item.description: Apple's macOS Sequoia 15.2 update has introduced changes that prevent certain third-party apps from creating bootable backups, frustrating users who rely on these tools for data security.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia might reveal DLSS 4 at CES 2025 – and mysterious new AI capabilities that could be ‘revolutionary’ for GPUs
item.description: Inno3D has been leaking info about Nvidia’s next-gen products again, with some eye-opening revelations here.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A new neutrino detector in China aims to spot mysterious ghost particles lurking around us
item.description: Scientists in China have almost finished building a massive underground detector to sniff out mysterious particles called neutrinos.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan And Ng Han Guan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: How Anyscale cofounder Robert Nishihara is smoothing AI’s complexities
item.description: As businesses reinvent workflows and products with AI, many such initiatives have stalled or failed to reach production. It can be really hard to implement the technology.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications
item.description: New realities of emergency work for emergency responders has necessitated increased use of data transmission of live incident video and file sharing. The post DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Using VPNs without messing up your banking apps
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a VPN enhances online banking security by encrypting data and protecting privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenTelemetry: The Open-Source Standard Poised to Revolutionize IT Infrastructure
item.description: OpenTelemetry, an open-source project by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation, is gaining momentum and poised to become a standard component of the IT infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US moves to tighten restrictions on China Telecom amid security fears
item.description: The US Commerce Department is intensifying its actions against China Telecom’s US unit over concerns that its cloud and internet services could be used to funnel American data to Beijing, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents
item.description: The draft national cyber incident response plan comes eight years after the last update, and more than six years after the creation of CISA itself. The post CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 18 indispensable Android travel apps
item.description: For all the ways travel’s evolved over the years, one thing has remained maddeningly steadfast: Moving from one place to another is almost always a hassle. There’s endless room for inconvenience and error, and a journey rarely goes according to plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 12 easy, techy ways to make the holiday smoother
item.description: To make the busiest time of year more manageable, here are some tricks for tracking your packages, taking quality family photos and curating the perfect Christmas playlist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Global internet traffic surges 17% as post-quantum cryptography adoption grows
item.description: Overall internet traffic continues to grow year after year, with no slowdown in sight. Cloudflare released its annual Cloudflare Radar Year in Review report this week, providing insights into the state of the internet in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sweden gets one of the EU’s seven AI factories
item.description: The new Swedish AI factory is named Mimer and is one of seven now selected by the EU. Mimer is one of five of these AI factories that will now have a brand new AI-optimized supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Network World
